payload.text: A radioactive isotope has a decay constant of 0.0237 per year. A sample starts with 214.3 grams. How many grams remain after 51.679 years?

Radioactive decay: N = N₀ * e^(-λt)

N = 214.3 * e^(-0.0237 * 51.679)

input.content:
import math
N0 = 214.3
lam = 0.0237
t = 51.679
N = N0 * math.exp(-lam * t)
print(round(N, 4))

payload.name: bash
payload.content:
exit=0
62.9654
Could not find platform independent libraries <prefix>

payload.text:
Using the exponential decay formula:

$$N = N_0 \, e^{-\lambda t} = 214.3 \times e^{-0.0237 \times 51.679} = 214.3 \times e^{-1.2248} \approx 62.9654 \text{ grams}$$

ANSWER: 62.9654

Using the exponential decay formula:

$$N = N_0 \, e^{-\lambda t} = 214.3 \times e^{-0.0237 \times 51.679} = 214.3 \times e^{-1.2248} \approx 62.9654 \text{ grams}$$

ANSWER: 62.9654
